plteams.name: Man City
plteams.about: Manchester City is one of England's most successful football clubs, founded in 1880 in the heart of Manchester. The club has achieved remarkable success in domestic and European football over the past decade. City are renowned for their attacking style of play, fluid passing, and world-class players across every position. Under Pep Guardiola, the team has dominated English football with multiple consecutive Premier League titles. The club's state-of-the-art facilities and academy continue to develop top talent while competing at the highest level every season.

plteams.name: Man United
plteams.about: Manchester United is one of the most iconic and successful football clubs in the world, founded in 1878 as Newton Heath. The club became a global brand under the legendary management of Sir Alex Ferguson, winning 13 Premier League titles. Old Trafford, known as the Theatre of Dreams, is one of the most recognizable stadiums in world football. United have produced some of the greatest players in football history including Cantona, Beckham, Ronaldo, and Rooney. The club continues to be a powerhouse in English football with a passionate global fanbase spanning over 200 countries.

plteams.name: Liverpool
plteams.about: Liverpool FC is one of the most decorated clubs in English and European football history, founded in 1892. The club is famous for its passionate supporters and the iconic anthem You'll Never Walk Alone, sung at every home match. Anfield is widely regarded as one of the most atmospheric stadiums in world football. Liverpool have won six European Cups and were Premier League champions in 2019-20 under Jurgen Klopp. The club continues its winning tradition under new manager Arne Slot, pushing for honours in every competition they enter.

plteams.name: Arsenal
plteams.about: Arsenal Football Club was founded in 1886 in Woolwich and has grown into one of England's most storied clubs. The Gunners are famous for their Invincibles season in 2003-04 when they went the entire Premier League campaign unbeaten. Mikel Arteta has rebuilt the squad into Premier League title contenders with an exciting brand of attacking football. The Emirates Stadium, opened in 2006, is a modern 60,000-capacity ground in North London. Arsenal hold the record for most FA Cup wins and continue to be a dominant force in English football.

plteams.name: Chelsea
plteams.about: Chelsea Football Club was founded in 1905 and is based in Fulham, West London at the historic Stamford Bridge stadium. The Roman Abramovich era transformed Chelsea into one of Europe's elite clubs, delivering multiple Premier League and Champions League titles. The Blues have consistently invested heavily in world-class talent and coaching over the past two decades. Under new ownership and manager Enzo Maresca, Chelsea are rebuilding with a focus on young, dynamic players. The club boasts a passionate fanbase and remains one of the most recognizable football brands in the world.

plteams.name: Tottenham
plteams.about: Tottenham Hotspur was founded in 1882 and is one of North London's most celebrated football clubs. Spurs play at the state-of-the-art Tottenham Hotspur Stadium, one of the finest football venues in the world opened in 2019. The club has a rich history of attractive, attacking football and produced legends like Jimmy Greaves and Glenn Hoddle. Ange Postecoglou brought his bold attacking philosophy to the club aiming to deliver long-awaited silverware. Spurs are one of the Premier League's most supported clubs globally and compete regularly in European competition.

plteams.name: Newcastle
plteams.about: Newcastle United was founded in 1892 and has one of the most passionate supporter cultures in English football. St. James' Park sits in the heart of the city and creates an electric atmosphere for every home match. The Saudi-led takeover in 2021 has brought significant investment, turning Newcastle into genuine Premier League contenders. Eddie Howe has transformed the squad with impressive signings and a high-energy pressing style of play. The club has a proud history and its fans, known as the Toon Army, are considered among the most loyal in the country.

plteams.name: Aston Villa
plteams.about: Aston Villa is one of England's oldest and most storied football clubs, founded in 1874 in Birmingham. The club famously won the European Cup in 1982, cementing their place in football history. Villa Park is a grand and historic stadium that has hosted FA Cup semi-finals and England internationals. Under Unai Emery, Aston Villa have returned to Champions League football and are competing with the best teams in Europe. The club's passionate fanbase, the Holte End faithful, create a tremendous atmosphere and drive the team forward every season.

plteams.name: West Ham
plteams.about: West Ham United was founded in 1895 and has a proud tradition of producing world-class English footballers. Known as the Academy of Football, the club gave the world Moore, Hurst, and Peters — the trio at the heart of England's 1966 World Cup triumph. The Hammers moved to the modern London Stadium in 2016, increasing their capacity to over 62,000. West Ham won the UEFA Europa Conference League in 2023, their first major European trophy. The club continues to develop young talent while competing strongly in the Premier League every season.

plteams.name: Brighton
plteams.about: Brighton & Hove Albion was founded in 1901 and has risen dramatically through the football pyramid to become an established Premier League club. The Seagulls are widely admired for their intelligent recruitment model and data-driven approach to player development. Under Graham Potter and then Roberto De Zerbi, Brighton played some of the most attractive attacking football in the Premier League. The Amex Stadium is a modern, comfortable ground with a fantastic atmosphere on matchdays. Brighton continue to punch well above their weight and regularly compete for European qualification spots.

plteams.name: Wolverhampton
plteams.about: Wolverhampton Wanderers was founded in 1877 and is one of England's oldest professional football clubs with a rich history. Wolves were dominant in the 1950s, winning three First Division titles and becoming one of Europe's first clubs to play under floodlights. The club made a stunning return to the Premier League in 2018 under Nuno Espirito Santo and has established itself as a consistent top-flight side. Molineux is a compact and passionate ground where the famous gold-shirted players take on the Premier League's best. Wolves continue to invest wisely in their squad and compete effectively in the modern game.

plteams.name: Crystal Palace
plteams.about: Crystal Palace was founded in 1905 and has been a fixture of the Premier League for over three decades. The Eagles are known for their passionate supporters and the iconic Holmesdale Road end which generates an incredible atmosphere. Selhurst Park is one of English football's most intimidating venues for visiting teams. Oliver Glasner led the club to impressive performances with an attacking and energetic brand of football. Crystal Palace pride themselves on developing young talent and competing with larger clubs despite limited resources, making them a beloved underdog of the Premier League.

plteams.name: Fulham
plteams.about: Fulham Football Club was founded in 1879, making it the oldest football club in London still playing professional football. Craven Cottage is one of English football's most charming and picturesque grounds, sitting alongside the River Thames in West London. The club yo-yoed between the Premier League and Championship for years before establishing itself as a stable top-flight side under Marco Silva. Fulham are known for their stylish passing football and smart recruitment of experienced international players. The Cottagers have a loyal and passionate fanbase who create a wonderful matchday atmosphere at their historic riverside home.

plteams.name: Everton
plteams.about: Everton Football Club was founded in 1878 and is one of the founding members of the Football League. The Toffees have spent more seasons in the top flight of English football than any other club, a proud record they fiercely defend. Goodison Park has been their beloved home since 1892 and remains one of English football's most atmospheric grounds. Everton are preparing to move into a spectacular new stadium at Bramley-Moore Dock on the Liverpool waterfront. The club has a passionate and loyal fanbase, the Evertonians, who remain devoted through the highs and lows of modern football.

plteams.name: Brentford
plteams.about: Brentford Football Club was founded in 1889 and has become one of English football's most fascinating modern success stories. After 74 years outside the top flight, the Bees earned promotion to the Premier League in 2021 under the visionary management of Thomas Frank. The club is renowned for their innovative data-driven recruitment strategy that finds value where others see none. Their compact Gtech Community Stadium creates an intense and noisy atmosphere that unsettles many top Premier League sides. Brentford continue to defy expectations each season, punching well above their weight against clubs with far greater resources.

plteams.name: Bournemouth
plteams.about: AFC Bournemouth was founded in 1899 and has experienced a remarkable rise from the lower leagues to become an established Premier League club. The Cherries spent five consecutive seasons in the top flight from 2015, defying the odds with their limited stadium capacity and resources. Andoni Iraola has brought an energetic and tactically astute brand of football to the south coast club. The Vitality Stadium is one of the Premier League's smallest grounds but generates a tremendous atmosphere on matchdays. Bournemouth are a model of efficient football management, consistently competing and developing quality players on a modest budget.

plteams.name: Nottingham
plteams.about: Nottingham Forest is one of English football's most historic clubs, founded in 1865 and two-time European Cup winners in 1979 and 1980. Under the legendary Brian Clough, Forest achieved one of sport's greatest overachievements by conquering Europe as a newly promoted side. The City Ground sits beside the River Trent and is one of English football's most iconic and atmospheric venues. After 23 years away, Forest returned to the Premier League in 2022 and have established themselves as a top-flight club once more. The club's passionate fanbase remains deeply proud of their extraordinary European heritage and history.

plteams.name: Leicester City
plteams.about: Leicester City was founded in 1884 and achieved the greatest underdog story in football history by winning the Premier League title in 2015-16 against odds of 5000-1. The Foxes had been battling relegation the previous season before a miraculous transformation under Claudio Ranieri captured the hearts of football fans worldwide. King Power Stadium is a modern, compact ground with a superb atmosphere created by their passionate fanbase. Leicester also won their first FA Cup in 2021, adding further silverware to their historic trophy cabinet. The club continues to rebuild and push for Premier League survival and future success under new management.

plteams.name: Ipswich Town
plteams.about: Ipswich Town was founded in 1878 and has a proud history including a First Division title in 1962 and a UEFA Cup triumph in 1981 under Bobby Robson. The club endured a long exile from the top flight before Kieran McKenna masterminded back-to-back promotions to return them to the Premier League. Portman Road is a traditional English ground with a warm and welcoming atmosphere in the heart of Suffolk. McKenna's exciting brand of attacking football has made Ipswich one of the most admired teams in the country. The Tractor Boys are hugely popular in East Anglia and their passionate supporters have fully embraced their top-flight return.

plteams.name: Southampton
plteams.about: Southampton Football Club was founded in 1885 and has a proud tradition of developing world-class footballers through their celebrated academy. The Saints have produced remarkable talents including Alan Shearer, Gareth Bale, Theo Walcott, and Luke Shaw who all came through their renowned youth system. St Mary's Stadium is a modern 32,000-capacity ground in the city centre that replaced the historic Dell in 2001. Southampton have experienced periods of Premier League stability and also fought against relegation in recent seasons. The club remains committed to their academy philosophy and community values that have defined them for over a century.

laligateams.name: Real Madrid
laligateams.about: Real Madrid is the most decorated football club in the world, founded in 1902 in the Spanish capital. The club has won a record 15 UEFA Champions League titles, cementing their status as the kings of European football. The Santiago Bernabéu Stadium is one of the most iconic sporting venues on the planet, recently renovated into a futuristic masterpiece. Under Carlo Ancelotti, Real Madrid continue to attract the world's best players and deliver memorable European nights. Their Galácticos policy of signing global superstars has made them the most valuable and recognizable football brand in the world.

laligateams.name: FC Barcelona
laligateams.about: FC Barcelona was founded in 1899 by Swiss businessman Joan Gamper and stands as one of the world's greatest football institutions. The club's philosophy of Més que un club — More than a club — reflects its deep cultural and political identity in Catalonia. Barcelona produced arguably the greatest footballer of all time in Lionel Messi, who broke every record imaginable during his two decades at the club. The iconic Camp Nou, currently under renovation, is the largest stadium in Europe with a capacity of over 99,000. Under Hansi Flick, Barça are rebuilding with exciting young talent and aiming to reclaim their dominance in Spain and Europe.

laligateams.name: Atletico Madrid
laligateams.about: Atletico Madrid was founded in 1903 and has built a fearsome reputation as one of Europe's most competitive and resilient clubs. Under Diego Simeone, the longest-serving manager among Europe's top clubs, Atleti have won two La Liga titles and reached two Champions League finals. The Civitas Metropolitano is a modern, stunning stadium that opened in 2017 and regularly creates an intimidating atmosphere. Known for their defensive solidity, pressing intensity, and never-say-die attitude, Atletico represent the ultimate fighting spirit in football. Their passionate fanbase fills the Metropolitano every week creating one of the most intense atmospheres in European club football.

laligateams.name: Sevilla FC
laligateams.about: Sevilla FC was founded in 1890 and is the dominant force in Spanish football outside the traditional top two clubs. The club holds the record for most UEFA Europa League titles with seven triumphs, an extraordinary achievement in European football. The Ramón Sánchez-Pizjuán stadium is a passionate and intimidating venue that has hosted some of European football's greatest nights. Sevilla are renowned for their outstanding recruitment model, bringing in quality players and developing them into European stars. The club has a fierce city rivalry with Real Betis that makes the Seville derby one of the most intense in world football.

laligateams.name: Valencia CF
laligateams.about: Valencia CF was founded in 1919 and is one of Spain's most historically significant football clubs, based in Spain's third-largest city. The club reached consecutive Champions League finals in 2000 and 2001, establishing themselves among Europe's elite. Mestalla is one of Spain's oldest and most atmospheric stadiums, known for creating an intense and electric matchday atmosphere. Valencia have a strong Copa del Rey record and have produced world-class talents including David Villa and David Silva. The club continues to compete in La Liga's upper echelons while working to restore their former European prominence.

laligateams.name: Villarreal CF
laligateams.about: Villarreal CF was founded in 1923 in the small city of Villarreal in the Valencia region, and has become one of Spain's most impressive sporting projects. The Yellow Submarine won the UEFA Europa League in 2021 under Unai Emery, beating Manchester United on penalties in a famous final. Despite coming from a city of only 50,000 people, Villarreal consistently compete in European competition each season. The Estadio de la Cerámica is an intimate and modern ground where the yellow-clad fans create a vibrant atmosphere. The club is widely admired for their smart recruitment, strong academy, and punching well above their weight in Spanish and European football.

laligateams.name: Real Sociedad
laligateams.about: Real Sociedad was founded in 1909 in the beautiful Basque city of San Sebastián and holds a special place in Spanish football culture. The club famously won back-to-back La Liga titles in 1981 and 1982 with a squad built primarily on Basque players. La Real are known for their commitment to developing homegrown talent through their excellent academy system. Imanol Alguacil has built a talented and cohesive squad that regularly competes in European competition. The club's Reale Arena stadium is a modern and impressive venue that creates a fantastic atmosphere in one of Spain's most picturesque cities.

laligateams.name: Athletic Club
laligateams.about: Athletic Club Bilbao was founded in 1898 and is one of world football's most unique institutions, fielding only players from the Basque Country. This extraordinary philosophy has never stopped them from competing at the highest level in Spanish football for over a century. San Mamés, known as the Cathedral of Football, is one of Spain's finest modern stadiums and creates an extraordinary atmosphere. Athletic have never been relegated from the top division of Spanish football, a record unmatched by any other club. The Copa del Rey is their most prized domestic trophy and the club won it in 2024, sending Bilbao into rapturous celebration.

laligateams.name: Real Betis
laligateams.about: Real Betis Balompié was founded in 1907 in Seville and is one of Spain's most beloved and passionate football clubs. The Verdiblancos have a deeply emotional fanbase who support the club through thick and thin across the Spanish football pyramid. The Benito Villamarín is a magnificent modern stadium with one of the loudest and most passionate atmospheres in La Liga. Under Manuel Pellegrini, Betis have become consistent top-six competitors and Europa League contenders in recent seasons. The club won the Copa del Rey in 2022, their first major trophy in decades, sparking incredible celebrations across the city of Seville.

laligateams.name: Girona FC
laligateams.about: Girona FC was founded in 1930 in the beautiful Catalan city of Girona and has risen to become one of Spanish football's most exciting stories. The club achieved a stunning third-place finish in La Liga 2023-24, qualifying for the Champions League for the first time in their history. Under manager Míchel, Girona play an electrifying and relentless brand of attacking football that has captured the imagination of the entire football world. Estadi Montilivi is one of La Liga's smallest grounds but creates an extraordinary and intimate atmosphere on big European nights. The club's remarkable rise is a testament to intelligent ownership, excellent coaching, and a clear footballing philosophy.

laligateams.name: Getafe CF
laligateams.about: Getafe CF was founded in 1983 in the suburban town of Getafe, just south of Madrid, and has established itself as a solid La Liga club against all odds. The team is famous for their highly organized, defensively disciplined, and physically intense style of play under their experienced manager José Bordalás. Coliseum Alfonso Pérez is a compact ground where the blue army of fans back their team with tremendous loyalty. Getafe have qualified for European competition on multiple occasions, a remarkable feat for a club of their size and resources. They are one of La Liga's most resilient sides, consistently surviving and competing despite operating with a modest budget.

laligateams.name: Osasuna
laligateams.about: Osasuna was founded in 1920 in the historic Navarrese city of Pamplona and is one of La Liga's most passionate and community-oriented clubs. The club is fan-owned and cooperatively run, making them one of Spain's most authentic and democratic sporting institutions. El Sadar is a compact, modern stadium where the Rojillo faithful create an intense and relentless atmosphere on matchdays. Osasuna are known for their gritty, hardworking style of football and team spirit that makes them difficult opponents for any side. The club made the Copa del Rey final in 2023, their deepest run in the competition, continuing to overachieve relative to their resources.

laligateams.name: Mallorca
laligateams.about: RCD Mallorca was founded in 1916 on the beautiful Balearic Island of Mallorca and has had an eventful history in Spanish football. The club enjoyed their greatest period in the late 1990s and early 2000s when they competed in European football under the ownership of Utz Claassen. Visit Mallorca Estadi is a modern stadium in the heart of Palma, the island's vibrant capital city. Mallorca are known for surviving in La Liga against more resourceful mainland clubs through hard work, team spirit, and tactical discipline. The club regularly attracts players with international experience and continues to develop a strong identity on and off the pitch.

laligateams.name: Celta Vigo
laligateams.about: Celta Vigo was founded in 1923 in the port city of Vigo in Galicia, northwestern Spain. The club is known for producing technically gifted players and playing attractive, attacking football throughout their history. Abanca-Balaídos is a passionate stadium with a strong backing from the loyal Galician faithful who support the club through the seasons. Celta have a proud history of competing in European football and have produced stars like Iago Aspas, one of La Liga's greatest modern strikers. The club continues to develop young talent and compete as a well-respected mid-table La Liga side with European ambitions.

laligateams.name: Las Palmas
laligateams.about: UD Las Palmas was founded in 1949 on the stunning island of Gran Canaria in the Canary Islands. The club has a passionate island fanbase who take immense pride in their team representing the Canaries on the national stage. Estadio Gran Canaria is a modern, comfortable venue where the yellow-clad supporters create a lively and festive atmosphere. Las Palmas returned to La Liga in 2023 after years in the lower divisions, bringing top-flight football back to the island. The club plays an attractive passing style of football that reflects their sunny island identity and continues to defy expectations in the Spanish top flight.

laligateams.name: Rayo Vallecano
laligateams.about: Rayo Vallecano was founded in 1924 in the working-class Vallecas neighbourhood of Madrid and is one of Spanish football's most politically conscious and community-driven clubs. The club is famous for their strong left-wing identity and social activism, refusing to be disconnected from the neighbourhood they represent. Estadio de Vallecas is one of Spain's most intimate and atmospheric grounds, creating a ferocious atmosphere just metres from the pitch. Rayo have punched well above their weight in La Liga despite having one of the smallest stadiums in the division. The club's passionate and politically engaged fanbase make them one of Spanish football's most unique and culturally important institutions.

laligateams.name: Deportivo Alavés
laligateams.about: Deportivo Alavés was founded in 1921 in the Basque city of Vitoria-Gasteiz and is famous for one of the most remarkable European runs in football history. The club reached the 2001 UEFA Cup Final, defeating several high-profile clubs along the way before losing 5-4 to Liverpool in a famous golden goal final. Estadio de Mendizorrotza is a compact and passionate Basque ground that creates tremendous noise on matchdays. Alavés are a proud yo-yo club that has spent time in both La Liga and the lower Spanish divisions throughout their history. The club draws deeply on Basque football culture and community pride to fuel their competitive spirit every season.

laligateams.name: Mallorca
laligateams.about: RCD Mallorca was founded in 1916 on the beautiful Balearic Island of Mallorca and has had an eventful history in Spanish football. The club enjoyed their greatest period in the late 1990s and early 2000s when they competed in European football under the ownership of Utz Claassen. Visit Mallorca Estadi is a modern stadium in the heart of Palma, the island's vibrant capital city. Mallorca are known for surviving in La Liga against more resourceful mainland clubs through hard work, team spirit, and tactical discipline. The club regularly attracts players with international experience and continues to develop a strong identity on and off the pitch.

laligateams.name: Alavés
laligateams.about: Deportivo Alavés was founded in 1921 in the Basque city of Vitoria-Gasteiz and is famous for one of the most remarkable European runs in football history. The club reached the 2001 UEFA Cup Final, defeating several high-profile clubs along the way before losing 5-4 to Liverpool in a famous golden goal final. Estadio de Mendizorrotza is a compact and passionate Basque ground that creates tremendous noise on matchdays. Alavés are a proud yo-yo club that has spent time in both La Liga and the lower Spanish divisions throughout their history. The club draws deeply on Basque football culture and community pride to fuel their competitive spirit every season.

laligateams.name: Espanyol
laligateams.about: RCD Espanyol was founded in 1900 and holds the distinction of being the second club of Barcelona, competing in the shadow of the mighty FC Barcelona. The club has a proud and distinct identity from their city rivals, drawing on their own loyal and passionate fanbase. Stage Front Stadium is a modern, impressive venue in the Cornellà suburb of Barcelona with strong transport links. Espanyol have reached two UEFA Cup Finals and have a solid European pedigree despite being overshadowed domestically. The club prides itself on developing Spanish talent and maintaining a unique Catalan identity that sets them apart in a city dominated by one footballing giant.

laligateams.name: Leganés
laligateams.about: CD Leganés was founded in 1928 in the Madrid suburb of Leganés and returned to La Liga in 2024 after several years in the second division. The Pepineros are a true community club with modest resources but a tremendous fighting spirit that endears them to neutral fans across Spain. Estadio Municipal de Butarque is one of La Liga's smallest venues, creating an intimate and loud atmosphere that gives the home team a genuine advantage. Leganés are renowned for their defensive organisation and collective team effort that makes them difficult to break down. The club's return to the top flight is celebrated by their passionate local community who have supported them through all divisions.

laligateams.name: Valladolid
laligateams.about: Real Valladolid was founded in 1928 in the Castilian city of Valladolid and gained worldwide attention when Brazilian legend Ronaldo purchased the club in 2018. The club has spent most of its history yo-yoing between La Liga and the Segunda División, giving them a resilient and determined character. Estadio José Zorrilla is a traditional Spanish football ground with a warm and welcoming atmosphere in the heart of Castile. Valladolid have always relied on finding undervalued players and developing a collective team spirit to compete against bigger clubs. The club continues to fight for La Liga survival each season, representing the city with immense local pride and community spirit.

serieaTeams.name: Napoli
serieaTeams.about: SSC Napoli is the soul of southern Italian football, representing the passionate city of Naples with fierce pride since their founding in 1926. The club's greatest icon is Diego Maradona, who led them to two historic Serie A titles in 1987 and 1990 and has their stadium named in his eternal honour. Antonio Conte guided Napoli to the 2024-25 Serie A title, delivering back-to-back championship success following their dominant 2022-23 triumph. The atmosphere at the Maradona stadium is among the most intense and emotional in world football, fuelled by the city's legendary passion for the game. Napoli are champions of Italy and one of Europe's most captivating and exciting clubs.

serieaTeams.name: Inter Milan
serieaTeams.about: FC Internazionale Milano is one of Italy's most decorated clubs, having won 20 Serie A titles and three European Cups including their iconic 2010 treble under José Mourinho. Founded in 1908 as a breakaway from AC Milan, Inter have always represented an international spirit reflected in their very name. The San Siro is a cathedral of Italian football shared with city rivals AC Milan and one of the world's most iconic sporting venues. Under Simone Inzaghi, Inter have been consistent Serie A title challengers playing attractive, high-intensity football. Lautaro Martínez and the Nerazzurri faithful make the Milanese club a constant European powerhouse.

serieaTeams.name: AC Milan
serieaTeams.about: AC Milan is one of world football's true aristocrats, boasting seven European Cup and Champions League titles — a record they share only with Real Madrid and Liverpool. Founded in 1899, the Rossoneri dominated European football in the late 1980s and early 1990s under Arrigo Sacchi and Fabio Capello with legendary players like Baresi, Maldini, and Van Basten. Their shared San Siro ground is one of football's greatest arenas, capable of generating earth-shaking noise when packed with passionate tifosi. Milan won the 2021-22 Serie A title under Stefano Pioli, ending an eleven-year drought. Under Sergio Conceição the club continues to battle for silverware and Champions League glory.

serieaTeams.name: Juventus
serieaTeams.about: Juventus is by far the most successful club in Italian football history, having accumulated 36 Serie A titles with a record nine consecutive championships from 2012 to 2020. Founded in 1897 in Turin, the Old Lady is synonymous with Italian football excellence, tactical discipline, and relentless winning mentality. Their modern Allianz Stadium is one of Italy's finest grounds, purpose-built for the club and opened in 2011. Legends such as Michel Platini, Roberto Baggio, Alessandro Del Piero, and Gianluigi Buffon have defined the club's DNA. Thiago Motta's appointment signals a new era of stylish, possession-based football at this most storied of clubs.

serieaTeams.name: Atalanta
serieaTeams.about: Atalanta BC is Italian football's greatest modern overachiever, a small-city club from Bergamo that has consistently competed with Italy's elite under the genius of Gian Piero Gasperini. Founded in 1907, the club became a European sensation after reaching the Champions League quarter-finals in 2020 and won the Europa League in 2024. Gasperini's relentless pressing and attacking system has produced some of Serie A's most thrilling football and unearthed remarkable talents. The renovated Gewiss Stadium creates an electric atmosphere despite its modest capacity, with the Curva Nord supporters among Italy's most passionate. Atalanta represent the very best of what intelligent, coherent football management can achieve.

serieaTeams.name: Fiorentina
serieaTeams.about: ACF Fiorentina is one of Italian football's most romantic and stylish clubs, based in the Renaissance city of Florence and playing in their iconic violet strip since 1926. The club won two Serie A titles and was a dominant force in Italian football during the 1950s and 1960s with legends like Gabriel Batistuta representing them in the modern era. The Stadio Artemio Franchi is a historic and architecturally significant ground currently undergoing renovation to restore it to former glory. Under Raffaele Palladino, Moise Kean has flourished as a top scorer and the club reached the Conference League semi-finals. Fiorentina have one of the most passionate and devoted fanbases in all of Italian football.

serieaTeams.name: Lazio
serieaTeams.about: SS Lazio is one of Rome's two great football clubs, founded in 1900 and fiercely proud of their sky-blue identity that sets them apart from city rivals AS Roma. The Eagles have won two Serie A titles and their 2000 championship under Sven-Göran Eriksson with heroes like Alessandro Nesta and Juan Sebastián Verón was truly spectacular. The Stadio Olimpico is shared with Roma and is one of Europe's most iconic multi-sport venues, hosting finals and international events throughout its history. Lazio have developed a passionate ultras culture and a fanbase that is among the most fervent in Italian football. Marco Baroni has brought energy and ambition back to the club in recent seasons.

serieaTeams.name: AS Roma
serieaTeams.about: AS Roma is the heart of Roman football, founded in 1927 and representing the eternal city with three Serie A titles and a passionate global following. The Giallorossi have experienced incredible European nights including their 2023 Europa League final appearance and Jose Mourinho's 2022 Conference League victory, Roma's first European trophy in 61 years. Club legend Claudio Ranieri returned to manage the club he loves in 2024, tasked with returning them to Champions League football. Francesco Totti, the prince of Rome, embodied the club for 25 years and remains the greatest icon of the capital's favourite team. Roma's devoted tifosi create one of Italian football's most electric and emotionally charged atmospheres.

serieaTeams.name: Bologna FC
serieaTeams.about: Bologna FC is one of Italy's oldest clubs, founded in 1909 in the gastronomic capital of Italy, and carrying seven Serie A titles from their golden era in the 1930s and 1940s. The club made a sensational return to Champions League football in 2024-25 under Thiago Motta before his departure to Juventus, continuing their European adventure under Vincenzo Italiano. Stadio Renato Dall'Ara is a historic old ground in the heart of Bologna with a passionate and knowledgeable fanbase. The club has consistently developed exciting Italian talent and embraced a modern possession-based approach to the game. Bologna represent the authentic spirit of Italian football, rooted in their community and city with deep historical pride.

serieaTeams.name: Torino FC
serieaTeams.about: Torino FC carries one of Italian football's most poignant and powerful stories, founded in 1906 and home to the legendary Grande Torino side of the 1940s who won five consecutive Serie A titles. The entire first team perished in the Superga air disaster in 1949 — a tragedy that still defines the club's soul and inspires annual commemorations by the devoted Toro faithful. The club shares Turin with more decorated neighbours Juventus but maintains fierce independence and a proud working-class identity. Their Stadio Olimpico Grande Torino is named in honour of those lost heroes of 1949. Torino remain an important and beloved institution in Italian football with a deeply emotional connection to their city.

serieaTeams.name: Udinese
serieaTeams.about: Udinese Calcio is one of Italian football's most established clubs, founded in 1896 in the northeastern city of Udine close to the borders of Austria and Slovenia. The club has been a consistent Serie A presence for decades, famous for their remarkable talent-spotting ability in discovering and developing world-class players from around the globe. Alumni of Udinese include Oliver Bierhoff, Alexis Sánchez, and Juan Cuadrado who all used the club as a springboard to European stardom. The Bluenergy Stadium is a modern arena with a loyal local fanbase who appreciate their club's intelligent and sustainable approach to football. Udinese represent the best of provincial Italian football — consistent, smart, and deeply respected.

serieaTeams.name: Genoa CFC
serieaTeams.about: Genoa CFC holds the remarkable distinction of being Italy's oldest football club, founded in 1893 by English expatriates in the historic port city of Genoa. The Grifone won nine Italian championships before the Second World War and carry a proud and illustrious history in Italian football. The Stadio Luigi Ferraris is a wonderful old stadium shared with local rivals Sampdoria and one of Italian football's most characterful grounds. Under Patrick Vieira the club is developing an attractive modern style while maintaining their rich traditions. Genoa's devoted fanbase, the Gradinata Nord ultras, are among Italy's most colourful and passionate supporter groups.

serieaTeams.name: Cagliari
serieaTeams.about: Cagliari Calcio is the pride of Sardinia, founded in 1920 and representing the island with passionate local pride in every Serie A campaign. The club's greatest achievement came in 1970 when they won the Serie A title led by the legendary Gigi Riva, Sardinia's greatest sporting hero. The modern Unipol Domus opened in 2023 and provides a compact but atmospheric home with one of Italian football's most distinctive island settings. Cagliari have battled relegation and fought their way back to Serie A multiple times, reflecting the resilience and fighting spirit of their devoted supporters. The Rossoblu are an essential part of Italian football's fabric and a club deeply intertwined with Sardinian identity.

serieaTeams.name: Hellas Verona
serieaTeams.about: Hellas Verona FC is one of Italian football's great historical clubs, founded in 1903 in the romantic northern Italian city of Shakespeare's Romeo and Juliet. Their greatest triumph came in the miraculous 1984-85 season when the club won the Serie A title under Osvaldo Bagnoli with a team of journeymen players, one of Italian football's greatest ever upsets. The Stadio Marcantonio Bentegodi is shared with Chievo and has a vibrant if sometimes turbulent atmosphere on matchdays. Verona yo-yo between Serie A and Serie B regularly, always fighting hard to maintain top-flight status. The passionate Hellas ultras are among Italian football's most colourful and committed supporter groups.

serieaTeams.name: Empoli FC
serieaTeams.about: Empoli FC is a Tuscan club from the small city of Empoli between Florence and Pisa, founded in 1920 with a long tradition of producing Italian football talent through their highly regarded youth academy. The club has become a model in Italian football for their player development philosophy, sending dozens of players on to Serie A's bigger clubs and the Italian national team. Their compact Stadio Carlo Castellani creates a passionate local atmosphere in the heart of Tuscany. Empoli consistently survive in Serie A with limited resources through intelligent coaching and smart recruitment of loan players. Under Roberto D'Aversa the club continues their admirable tradition of competing with discipline and tactical intelligence.

serieaTeams.name: Lecce
serieaTeams.about: US Lecce is the pride of Puglia, representing the beautiful Baroque city of Lecce in the heel of Italy's boot since their founding in 1908. Known as the Giallorossi of the South, Lecce have established themselves as one of southern Italy's most consistent Serie A clubs in recent years. Their Stadio Via del Mare is a characterful ground bathed in the warmth of the Salento sun with passionate local supporters creating a festive atmosphere. The club is renowned for developing young attacking talent and playing an entertaining brand of football. Lecce's fighting spirit and community pride make them one of Serie A's most sympathetic and popular provincial clubs.

serieaTeams.name: Parma
serieaTeams.about: Parma Calcio is one of Italian football's most romantic clubs, enjoying a glittering period in the 1990s when they won three Coppa Italia titles and three UEFA trophies including the Cup Winners' Cup and UEFA Cup. Founded in 1913 in the food capital of Italy, Parma's golden years brought stars like Gianluigi Buffon, Fabio Cannavaro, and Lilian Thuram before their dramatic financial collapse. The club remarkably rebuilt themselves from the fourth division and returned to Serie A, one of football's greatest survival stories. The Stadio Ennio Tardini is a historic ground with a devoted fanbase celebrating their club's extraordinary resurrection. Parma are back where they belong and determined to write exciting new chapters in their storied history.

serieaTeams.name: Como 1907
serieaTeams.about: Como 1907 is Italian football's most glamorous story of recent times, returning to Serie A after 21 years and playing under the management of Arsenal and Barcelona legend Cesc Fàbregas. Founded in 1907 on the stunning shores of Lake Como, the club has been transformed by ambitious Indonesian ownership into a genuine Serie A force. The picturesque Stadio Sinigaglia sits directly beside Lake Como, arguably the most beautiful stadium setting in Italian football. Fàbregas has recruited an impressive roster of experienced internationals while developing young talent in his exciting project. Como represent the perfect blend of historic Italian football tradition and ambitious modern vision under their charismatic young manager.

serieaTeams.name: Sassuolo
serieaTeams.about: Sassuolo is one of the most modern and exciting football clubs in Italian football, founded in 1920 and rising rapidly through the divisions during the 21st century. The club became famous for its attacking style of play and smart player development strategy that transformed them into a respected Serie A side. Sassuolo play their home matches at the modern Mapei Stadium in Reggio Emilia, known for its excellent facilities and atmosphere. The Neroverdi have earned admiration across Italy for competing against bigger clubs with attractive football and talented young players. Sassuolo continues to represent ambition, growth, and modern football culture in Serie A.

serieaTeams.name: Monza
serieaTeams.about: AC Monza achieved a dream come true in 2022 when they reached Serie A for the first time in their 110-year history under the ownership of former Italian Prime Minister Silvio Berlusconi and CEO Adriano Galliani. Founded in 1912, the club from the racing city of Monza beside Formula One's famous Autodromo has embraced top-flight football with ambitious signings and a modern approach. Football legend Alessandro Nesta, himself a former Serie A champion, has taken charge of guiding the club through this exciting new chapter. The Stadio Brianteo is a compact ground that generates tremendous noise and support from the passionate Brianzoli faithful. Monza are a wonderful addition to Serie A and continue their fairy-tale journey at the highest level.

bundesligaTeams.name: Bayern Munich
bundesligaTeams.about: FC Bayern Munich is Germany's most successful club by a vast margin, having won 33 Bundesliga titles including a record eleven consecutive championships from 2013 to 2023. Founded in 1900, Bayern are a global footballing superpower and the only club to have won the Champions League, Bundesliga, and DFB-Pokal treble twice. The futuristic Allianz Arena is one of world football's most iconic and technologically advanced stadiums, lit up in club colours on matchday. Legends like Franz Beckenbauer, Gerd Müller, Oliver Kahn, and Robert Lewandowski have defined generations of Bayern excellence. Under Vincent Kompany, Bayern are determined to recapture Bundesliga supremacy with an exciting new generation of talent.

bundesligaTeams.name: Dortmund
bundesligaTeams.about: Borussia Dortmund is Germany's most atmospheric club, home to the legendary Yellow Wall — the Südtribüne terrace holding 24,000 standing fans and the largest single terrace in world football. Founded in 1909, BVB won eight Bundesliga titles including back-to-back championships under Jürgen Klopp in 2011 and 2012. Signal Iduna Park is Germany's largest stadium and creates a spine-tingling atmosphere that opposition clubs consistently name as the toughest venue in European football. Dortmund reached the 2024 Champions League final, losing narrowly to Real Madrid in a devastating defeat at Wembley. The club's passionate working-class fanbase and yellow-black identity make them one of world football's most beloved clubs.

bundesligaTeams.name: Leverkusen
bundesligaTeams.about: Bayer Leverkusen made history in 2023-24 by winning their first ever Bundesliga title under the brilliant management of Xabi Alonso, going the entire season unbeaten in one of modern football's greatest achievements. Founded in 1904 as a sports club for workers of the Bayer pharmaceutical company, Die Werkself are now one of German football's elite sides. The compact BayArena creates an intense atmosphere for European nights and Bundesliga fixtures in the industrial Rhineland city. Xabi Alonso's elegant possession-based system has attracted global admiration and produced some of Germany's most entertaining football. Leverkusen also won the DFB-Pokal in 2024, completing a domestic double in their historic invincible season.

bundesligaTeams.name: RB Leipzig
bundesligaTeams.about: RB Leipzig is German football's most controversial and fascinating modern story, rising from the fifth division in 2009 to the Bundesliga in just seven years through Red Bull's financial backing. Despite fierce opposition from traditional football culture, Leipzig have established themselves as genuine title contenders and regular Champions League participants. Their high-energy gegenpressing system has been developed through a global network of Red Bull clubs sharing coaching philosophies and players. The Red Bull Arena is a modern and impressive stadium in Germany's cultural music city, attracting strong attendances from a rapidly growing fanbase. Leipzig won the DFB-Pokal in 2022 and continue pushing Bayern Munich and Dortmund for Bundesliga supremacy.

bundesligaTeams.name: Frankfurt
bundesligaTeams.about: Eintracht Frankfurt is one of German football's most beloved clubs, with a rich history dating to 1899 and a passionate fanbase known for their extraordinary European following. The Eagles achieved one of the Bundesliga era's greatest triumphs by winning the 2022 UEFA Europa League, with their famous travelling fans filling Seville's streets and the stadium with breathtaking numbers. Deutsche Bank Park is a 58,000-capacity stadium in Germany's financial capital that generates tremendous atmosphere for every European and domestic fixture. Frankfurt are known for developing attacking, risk-taking football that reflects the confident and cosmopolitan character of their city. Their devoted supporters make travelling to Frankfurt one of European football's most memorable experiences.

bundesligaTeams.name: VfB Stuttgart
bundesligaTeams.about: VfB Stuttgart is a proud Swabian club with 130 years of history, five Bundesliga titles, and a tradition of developing some of Germany's finest players. The club's Mercedes-Benz Arena, now MHPArena, has undergone major renovation and is one of Germany's most impressive modern stadiums. Under Sebastian Hoeneß, Stuttgart had a spectacular 2023-24 season finishing second in the Bundesliga and returning to Champions League football. Young talents like Deniz Undav and Enzo Millot flourished in an exciting attacking system that electrified the Bundesliga. Stuttgart represent the best of German football's proud regional identity, deeply connected to their industrial city and Swabian culture.

bundesligaTeams.name: SC Freiburg
bundesligaTeams.about: SC Freiburg is German football's most admired model club, located in the Black Forest city of Freiburg and operating sustainably for over two decades under the visionary management of Christian Streich until 2024. The club consistently competes in European football on one of the Bundesliga's smallest budgets through exceptional coaching, youth development, and a clear playing philosophy. Their Europa-Park Stadion is one of the world's most environmentally sustainable sports venues, featuring solar panels and ambitious green energy goals. New manager Julian Schuster continues the club's philosophy of developing German talent with coherent, organized attacking football. Freiburg represent the ideal of what football can achieve through intelligence and long-term thinking.

bundesligaTeams.name: Mönchengladbach
bundesligaTeams.about: Borussia Mönchengladbach is one of German football's most storied clubs, winning five Bundesliga titles and two UEFA Cup trophies during their golden 1970s era under Hennes Weisweiler. The Foals produced legends like Berti Vogts, Günter Netzer, and Jupp Heynckes who went on to shape German football history as players and managers. The modern Borussia-Park is a 54,000-capacity stadium with one of the Bundesliga's most passionate and devoted fanbases roaring their side on every matchday. The club has strong traditional rivalries with Cologne and Bayer Leverkusen that produce some of German football's most charged local derby atmospheres. Gladbach continue to develop young talent and compete consistently in the upper half of the Bundesliga table.

bundesligaTeams.name: Hoffenheim
bundesligaTeams.about: TSG 1899 Hoffenheim is one of German football's most fascinating modern projects, rising from the Bundesliga's lower reaches to become established top-flight members thanks to investment from software billionaire Dietmar Hopp. Founded in 1899 in the tiny village of Hoffenheim, the club now plays in the modern PreZero Arena near Sinsheim and consistently competes in European competition. The club produced influential managers including Julian Nagelsmann and Ralf Rangnick who shaped modern German football's tactical development. Christian Ilzer has continued the tradition of playing adventurous, attack-minded football with Andrej Kramarić as the consistent attacking threat. Hoffenheim serve as a model for ambitious smaller clubs seeking to compete sustainably in an elite European league.

bundesligaTeams.name: Bremen
bundesligaTeams.about: Werder Bremen is one of German football's great traditional clubs, four-time Bundesliga champions with a rich history dating back to 1899 in the historic Hanseatic city of Bremen. The club had a golden era in the 1980s and 1990s under Otto Rehhagel and produced legends like Karl-Heinz Riedle and Ailton. The Weserstadion sits beautifully beside the River Weser and creates one of the Bundesliga's warmest and most welcoming matchday atmospheres. After a painful relegation, Werder rebuilt and returned to Bundesliga football with great spirit under young coach Ole Werner. The Grün-Weißen faithful are among German football's most loyal supporters, filling the stadium week after week with passionate green-and-white colour.

bundesligaTeams.name: Wolfsburg
bundesligaTeams.about: VfL Wolfsburg is a company club founded in 1945 by Volkswagen workers and intimately connected to the automobile giant's global headquarters in Lower Saxony. The Wolves won their only Bundesliga title in the 2008-09 season under Felix Magath with Edin Džeko and Grafite forming one of the most prolific strike partnerships in German football history. The modern Volkswagen Arena is a state-of-the-art stadium in the car city of Wolfsburg with excellent facilities and a devoted fanbase. Wolfsburg are also one of women's football's most successful clubs globally, with multiple Champions League titles for their women's team. Under Ralph Hasenhüttl the men's team aims to return to the European competition that the club enjoyed in its recent history.

bundesligaTeams.name: Union Berlin
bundesligaTeams.about: 1. FC Union Berlin is one of world football's most unique and inspiring clubs, with deep roots in the working-class Köpenick district of East Berlin where the club was founded in 1906. Their Stadion An der Alten Försterei is a legendary ground where fans actually helped rebuild the stands with their own hands in 2008, embodying the extraordinary community spirit at the club's core. Union made history by reaching the Bundesliga in 2019 and remarkably qualified for the Champions League in 2023, achieving things that seemed impossible just years earlier. The club's famous candlelit Christmas carol singing tradition in the stadium captures the unique and emotional relationship between Union and their devoted supporters. The Eisernen are genuinely unlike any other club in European football.

bundesligaTeams.name: FC Augsburg
bundesligaTeams.about: FC Augsburg is a proud Swabian club from the historic Bavarian city of Augsburg, founded in 1907 and competing in the Bundesliga since 2011 as one of its most consistent smaller clubs. The WWK Arena is a compact and atmospheric stadium that has seen Augsburg consistently hold their own against Germany's biggest clubs through excellent organization and fighting spirit. The club are known for their resilient and well-drilled defensive style that makes them one of the Bundesliga's most difficult opponents. Augsburg have occasionally flirted with European qualification and produced several players who have gone on to represent the German national team. Their modest but consistent story of sustained top-flight survival is a model for provincial clubs across European football.

bundesligaTeams.name: FC Heidenheim
bundesligaTeams.about: 1. FC Heidenheim is German football's greatest recent underdog story, rising from amateur football to the Bundesliga under manager Frank Schmidt who has led the club for over 15 consecutive years of remarkable progress. The club is based in one of the Bundesliga's smallest towns and plays at the Voith-Arena, the highest altitude professional football ground in Germany at 555 metres above sea level. Schmidt's long-term commitment and consistent philosophy represent everything admirable in football management, building a cohesive identity and genuine club culture. Heidenheim have established themselves as competitive Bundesliga members despite having by far the smallest resources and stadium in the division. Their extraordinary story captures the imagination of football fans across Germany and beyond.

bundesligaTeams.name: FC St. Pauli
bundesligaTeams.about: FC St. Pauli is world football's most politically conscious and culturally distinctive club, based in Hamburg's bohemian St. Pauli neighbourhood with global cult status far exceeding their footballing achievements. Founded in 1910, the club adopted left-wing, anti-fascist values and the skull-and-crossbones logo that has become an international symbol of alternative football culture. The Millerntor-Stadion sits in the heart of Hamburg's famous Reeperbahn entertainment district and creates one of the most intense and colourful atmospheres in German football. St. Pauli returned to the Bundesliga in 2024 after years in the second division, celebrated passionately by their global fanbase. Their message that football belongs to everyone resonates with supporters on every continent who cherish the club's rebellious spirit.

bundesligaTeams.name: Holstein Kiel
bundesligaTeams.about: Holstein Kiel made history in 2024 by reaching the Bundesliga for the first time in their 124-year history, becoming the northernmost club ever to compete in Germany's top flight. Founded in 1900 in the Baltic Sea port city of Kiel, the Storks rose through the divisions with a spirited and coherent team under Marcel Rapp's young management. The compact Holstein-Stadion by the Kiel waterfront hosted top-flight football for the first time, creating scenes of extraordinary emotion and pride in the city. Kiel faced the enormous challenge of competing against established Bundesliga clubs with the division's smallest budget but brought infectious enthusiasm to every match. Their promotion achievement was one of German football's most celebrated stories in recent memory.

bundesligaTeams.name: VfL Bochum
bundesligaTeams.about: VfL Bochum is a Ruhr Valley club founded in 1848 with a proud working-class identity rooted in Germany's historic coal mining region. The Vonovia Ruhrstadion is a classic West German ground that has witnessed decades of top-flight football and retains genuine character and atmosphere. Bochum are one of the Bundesliga's most resilient clubs, having maintained their top-flight status through numerous challenges before their recent relegation struggle. The club has a deeply loyal and passionate fanbase from across the Ruhr region who maintain extraordinary commitment through promotion and relegation campaigns alike. Bochum's honest, hard-working football reflects the values of the mining communities they have represented for nearly 180 years of their remarkable history.

bundesligaTeams.name: Mainz 05
bundesligaTeams.about: 1. FSV Mainz 05 is one of the Bundesliga's most consistently entertaining clubs, having produced legendary managers in Jürgen Klopp and Thomas Tuchel who both launched their careers at the Rhineland club. Founded in 1905 in the Carnival city of Mainz, the club is famous for their lively and energetic matchday culture influenced by the city's famous Fassenacht celebrations. The modern MEWA Arena is a compact and excellent stadium with one of the Bundesliga's most enthusiastic and inventive supporter cultures. Under Bo Henriksen, Mainz play aggressive, high-intensity pressing football that consistently unsettles much larger clubs. The Nullfünfer are a model of how to be a perpetual top-flight club through consistency, smart recruitment, and outstanding coaching development.

ligue1Teams.name: Paris
ligue1Teams.about: Paris Saint-Germain is French football's dominant superpower and one of the world's most recognised clubs, transformed by Qatari Sports Investments' takeover in 2011 into a global brand. Founded in 1970, PSG have won 13 Ligue 1 titles and been the dominant force in French football for over a decade. The club attracted Neymar, Kylian Mbappé, and Lionel Messi in their blockbuster recruitment drives, creating one of world football's most expensive squads. The Parc des Princes is a beautiful compact stadium in the 16th arrondissement of Paris with an electric atmosphere fuelled by the passionate Auteuil and Boulogne supporter groups. Under Luis Enrique, PSG continue their relentless pursuit of the Champions League trophy that has eluded them despite their enormous resources.

ligue1Teams.name: Marseille
ligue1Teams.about: Olympique de Marseille is France's most passionate football club and the only French side to win the UEFA Champions League, their legendary 1993 triumph under Raymond Goethals remaining an iconic moment in European football history. Founded in 1899 in the vibrant port city of Marseille, OM inspire fanatical devotion from one of football's most emotional and colourful supporter cultures. The Orange Vélodrome is France's largest club stadium and creates spine-tingling noise when filled with 67,000 passionate Marseillais behind their beloved team. Roberto De Zerbi has brought his attacking, possession-based philosophy to the club creating exciting and dynamic football. The eternal rivalry with Paris Saint-Germain, Le Classique, is French football's defining fixture and one of Europe's most charged derby encounters.

ligue1Teams.name: Lyonnais
ligue1Teams.about: Olympique Lyonnais is one of French football's great institutions, winning an extraordinary seven consecutive Ligue 1 titles between 2002 and 2008 under president Jean-Michel Aulas. Founded in 1950, Lyon built their success on excellent youth development and smart recruitment while operating from their spectacular Groupama Stadium opened in 2016. The club has produced world-class talents including Karim Benzema, Alexandre Lacazette, Nabil Fekir, and Samuel Umtiti who all rose through their celebrated academy. Lyon are famous globally for their women's team which has dominated European football with multiple Champions League titles. Under Paulo Fonseca the men's team aims to return to the Champions League and restore their status as France's leading challengers to PSG.

ligue1Teams.name: AS Monaco
ligue1Teams.about: AS Monaco is one of European football's most glamorous clubs, based in the tiny tax-haven principality of Monaco overlooking the Mediterranean Sea. Founded in 1924 and backed by Russian billionaire Dmitry Rybolovlev, the club made history in 2016-17 by winning Ligue 1 and reaching the Champions League semi-finals with a breathtaking young squad including Kylian Mbappé, Bernardo Silva, and Thomas Lemar. The intimate Stade Louis II sits carved into the cliffs of Monte Carlo and creates a unique and exclusive matchday atmosphere. Monaco have won eight Ligue 1 titles and remain one of French football's most ambitious and well-resourced clubs. Adi Hütter has built a competitive and exciting team that regularly challenges for European qualification and domestic honours.

ligue1Teams.name: LOSC Lille
ligue1Teams.about: LOSC Lille is one of French football's most storied clubs, based in the northern industrial city of Lille and winning four Ligue 1 titles including their stunning 2020-21 championship that ended PSG's dominant run. Founded in 1944 through the merger of several clubs, Lille have developed a strong reputation for finding and developing talented players in their excellent academy. The impressive Stade Pierre-Mauroy was built for Euro 2016 and is one of France's finest modern stadiums with a passionate northern fanbase. Lille regularly punch above their weight and compete in Champions League and Europa League football through intelligent management and recruitment. Their devoted supporters, the Dogues faithful, create tremendous atmosphere in one of northern France's proudest sporting institutions.

ligue1Teams.name: RC Lens
ligue1Teams.about: RC Lens is French football's most passionately supported working-class club, representing the former coal mining communities of the Pas-de-Calais region with extraordinary devotion. Founded in 1906, the Blood and Gold have a fanbase that nearly matches the entire population of Lens itself, creating one of France's most intense and atmospheric matchday experiences. The historic Stade Bollaert-Delelis has a stadium-to-city-population ratio unmatched anywhere in European football, with the stadium holding more than the town's total residents. Lens finished second in Ligue 1 in 2022-23 under Franck Haise and qualified for the Champions League, their greatest achievement in the modern era. The miners' club remains French football's ultimate community institution with passionate supporters who travel in enormous numbers across France and Europe.

ligue1Teams.name: Rennais
ligue1Teams.about: Stade Rennais is the proud representative of Brittany in French football, founded in 1901 in the vibrant Breton capital of Rennes with deep cultural connections to the region's distinct Celtic identity. The club has experienced their most successful modern era in recent years, qualifying for the Champions League in 2022-23 and winning the Coupe de France in 2019 and 2022. Roazhon Park is a modern stadium with a colourful and passionate atmosphere created by the Breton faithful who proudly display their regional flags and banners. Jorge Sampaoli has brought ambitious and attacking football to the club while continuing their tradition of developing young French talent. Rennes are one of Ligue 1's most exciting and progressive clubs with an ambitious ownership group committed to European competition.

ligue1Teams.name: OGC Nice
ligue1Teams.about: OGC Nice is one of France's most glamorous clubs, representing the jewel of the French Riviera since their founding in 1904 in the beautiful Mediterranean city. The Eagles play at the stunning Allianz Riviera — one of France's finest modern stadiums with spectacular views of the surrounding hills, built for Euro 2016. Under British-American ownership the club has invested significantly in quality players and coaches to challenge Ligue 1's elite. Nice have won four Ligue 1 titles historically and under Franck Haise they are rebuilding their status as genuine European contenders. The combination of Nice's breathtaking coastal setting, ambitious owners, and passionate southern fanbase makes them one of France's most attractive football destinations.

ligue1Teams.name: RC Strasbourg
ligue1Teams.about: RC Strasbourg Alsace is the proud representative of France's Alsatian region, a city with a fascinating dual French-German cultural identity on the border between the two nations. Founded in 1906, the club won the Ligue 1 title in 1979 and has a passionate and distinctive Alsatian fanbase with strong regional pride and identity. The Stade de la Meinau is a characterful old ground in the heart of Strasbourg that creates tremendous atmosphere for European fixtures and local derbies. Strasbourg play in a city that is simultaneously the seat of the European Parliament, reflecting their unique position at the heart of European culture. Under Liam Rosenior the club aims to develop consistent Ligue 1 performances and challenge for European qualification.

ligue1Teams.name: Toulouse FC
ligue1Teams.about: Toulouse FC is the main club of France's Pink City, representing the vibrant university capital of the Occitanie region with their distinctive violet strip since the club's founding in 1970. After winning promotion from Ligue 2 in 2022, Toulouse won the Coupe de France in 2023 in a remarkable achievement for their first season back in the top flight. The Stadium de Toulouse is one of France's largest club grounds and hosted matches during Euro 2016 and the 1998 World Cup. The club's ambitious Spanish ownership group led by Damien Comolli has brought an exciting international vision to the club's development. Toulouse are a rapidly developing force in French football enjoying renewed momentum and European competition after their Coupe de France triumph.

ligue1Teams.name: Montpellier
ligue1Teams.about: Montpellier HSC achieved one of Ligue 1's greatest ever upsets when they won the 2011-12 title under René Girard, defeating powerhouses Paris Saint-Germain and Marseille in a genuinely thrilling championship race. Founded in 1974 in the Mediterranean university city of Montpellier, the club has maintained a consistent top-flight presence while staying true to their regional roots. The Stade de la Mosson is a large and colourful ground with passionate supporters from across the Languedoc region. The club has produced excellent French players through their youth academy and maintained a community-focused identity throughout their development. Jean-Louis Gasset has returned to bring stability and coherent football philosophy to this charming southern French club.

ligue1Teams.name: Le Havre
ligue1Teams.about: Le Havre AC holds the remarkable distinction of being the oldest football club in France, founded in 1872 by English sailors and merchants in the bustling Norman port city. The club is also historically significant as the birthplace of Kylian Mbappé, who began his football journey in their youth academy before moving to Monaco and then to global superstardom. Their modern Stade Océane opened in 2012 and sits beside the English Channel with a distinctive sail-inspired architectural design that reflects the city's maritime heritage. Le Havre returned to Ligue 1 in 2023 after years in the second division, bringing renewed excitement to their devoted Norman fanbase. The club's historical importance and connection to English football's origins makes them one of France's most culturally significant sporting institutions.

ligue1Teams.name: Reims
ligue1Teams.about: Stade de Reims is one of French football's most historically distinguished clubs, winning six Ligue 1 titles in the 1950s and 1960s and reaching the European Cup final twice during French football's golden era. Founded in 1931 in the Champagne capital of Reims, the club produced legendary French players and faced the great Real Madrid side in memorable European battles. After decades in the lower divisions, Reims remarkably rebuilt and returned to Ligue 1 as a consistently competitive and intelligent club under careful management. The compact Stade Auguste-Delaune creates an intimate and passionate atmosphere in the beautiful cathedral city. Reims represent French football's authentic provincial tradition and the possibility of rebuilding historic clubs through patience and smart leadership.

ligue1Teams.name: Nantes
ligue1Teams.about: FC Nantes is one of French football's most cherished and historically significant clubs, winning eight Ligue 1 titles and producing the famous passing style known as the Jeu à la Nantaise that influenced French football's development. Founded in 1943 in the Atlantic port city on the Loire estuary, the Canaries are famous for their rich tradition of producing exceptional French talent including Marcel Desailly, Didier Deschamps, and Claude Makélélé. The Stade de la Beaujoire is a large and passionate stadium hosting devoted yellow-and-green supporters through the decades. Nantes won the Coupe de France in 2022 under Antoine Kombouaré in an emotional triumph that brought the city enormous joy. The club remains one of French football's most authentic and beloved institutions with a distinguished history.

ligue1Teams.name: Angers SCO
ligue1Teams.about: Angers SCO is a proud Loire Valley club founded in 1919 in the beautiful château city of Angers, historically known as the Garden of France. The club returned to Ligue 1 in 2024 after promotion from Ligue 2, bringing renewed energy to their passionate local fanbase in the heart of France's wine country. The Stade Raymond Kopa is named in honour of legendary French footballer Raymond Kopa, one of the game's all-time greats who inspired generations of French players. Angers have a tradition of developing young French talent through their academy and playing attractive football despite limited resources. The club embodies French football's provincial spirit and their devoted Black and White supporters create a warm and welcoming matchday atmosphere.

ligue1Teams.name: AJ Auxerre
ligue1Teams.about: AJ Auxerre is one of French football's most romantic and historically significant clubs, built largely by the legendary Guy Roux who managed the club for an extraordinary 44 years across two stints producing exceptional talent. Founded in 1905 in the Burgundy wine region, Auxerre won Ligue 1 in 1996 and reached European semi-finals despite operating from a small city with modest resources. The compact Stade Abbé-Deschamps is an intimate and passionate ground where Auxerre created memories that are treasured in French football history. The club returned to Ligue 1 in 2024, celebrated by their devoted supporters as a long-awaited homecoming to the top flight. Auxerre represent French football's great provincial tradition and a model of how community clubs can achieve remarkable things with patience and vision.

ligue1Teams.name: Brest
ligue1Teams.about: Stade Brestois 29 is the proud representative of Brittany's largest city and France's major Atlantic naval base, founded in 1950 with a strong connection to their maritime community. The club achieved a remarkable third-place finish in Ligue 1 in 2023-24, their best ever result, which remarkably earned them qualification for the UEFA Champions League group stages. Playing at the tiny Stade Francis-Le Blé, the smallest ground in Ligue 1, Brest created an extraordinary underdog story that captured France's footballing imagination. Éric Roy has built a cohesive and disciplined team that maximizes every resource available to compete with much wealthier clubs. Brest are planning a new 15,000-seat stadium and continue to grow as one of French football's most inspiring modern success stories.

ligue1Teams.name: Saint-Étienne
ligue1Teams.about: AS Saint-Étienne is France's most decorated football club in history, having won a record ten Ligue 1 titles and six Coupe de France trophies during their legendary green dynasty of the 1960s and 1970s. Founded in 1919 in the industrial mining city of Saint-Étienne, Les Verts reached the European Cup final in 1976 and produced iconic players like Michel Platini and Dominique Rocheteau who defined French football's golden generation. The Stade Geoffroy-Guichard, known as the Chaudron or Cauldron, is one of French football's most electric and intimidating venues with extraordinary atmosphere from the passionate green faithful. After years in Ligue 2, Saint-Étienne returned to the top flight in 2024, celebrated with enormous emotion by a city that lives and breathes for their famous green team. The club's return is one of French football's most anticipated and celebrated stories.
